text: Consider recurrences: For n>1, T₁(n) = 4T₁(n/2) + T₂(n), T₂(n) = 5T₂(n/4) + Θ(log₂n). For n≤1, T₁(n)=T₂(n)=1. Which option is correct?

Let G(V,E) be an undirected edge-weighted graph. Let dₖ(u) denote weight of shortest path from s to u of length at most k. S1: For every k≥0 and u∈V, dₖ₊₁(u) ≤ dₖ(u). S2: For every (u,v)∈E, if (u,v) is part of a shortest path from s to v, then for every k≥0, dₖ(u) ≤ dₖ(v). Which is correct?

options.C: Both S1 and S2 are true
options.D: Neither S1 nor S2 is true

text: Let G(V,E) be a simple undirected graph. A vertex cover of G is V'⊆V such that for every (u,v)∈E, u∈V' or v∈V'. Let size of smallest vertex cover be k. Let S be any vertex cover of size k. For vertex v∈V, which constraint always ensures v∈S?

options.A: The degree of v is at least k+1
options.B: The vertex v is on a path of length k+1
options.C: The vertex v is on a cycle of length k+1
options.D: The vertex v is part of a clique of size k

text: Let G(V,E) be a simple undirected edge-weighted graph with unique edge weights. Which statement about MSTs of G is/are true?

options.A: In every cycle C of G, the edge with the largest weight in C is not in any MST
options.B: In every cycle C of G, the edge with the smallest weight in C is in every MST
options.C: For every vertex v∈V, the edge with the largest weight incident on v is not in any MST
options.D: For every vertex v∈V, the edge with the smallest weight incident on v is in every MST

text: Consider DFS on a DAG G(V,E). For edge (u,v)∈E, which option will NEVER be correct about discovery/finish times?
An undirected unweighted simple graph G is 2-colorable if there exists c:V→{0,1} such that for every (u,v)∈E, c(u)≠c(v). Which statement(s) about 2-colorable graphs is/are true?

options.A: If G is 2-colorable, G may contain cycles of odd length
options.B: If G is 2-colorable, G may contain cycles of even length
options.C: Testing 2-colorability runs in Θ(|V|+|E|) with adjacency list
options.D: Testing 2-colorability runs in Θ(|E|log|V|) with adjacency list
